subject.title: English B
subject.description:
**IB English B** is a **Group 2** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **language acquisition through five prescribed themes: identities, experiences, human ingenuity, social organization, and sharing the planet**, with a strong focus on **developing receptive, productive, and interactive communication skills in English as a second language**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Paper 1** | Productive skills - writing | 1 hour | 1 hour 15 minutes | 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Receptive skills - listening and reading comprehension | 1 hour 45 minutes | 2 hours | 50% |
| **Individual Oral** | Internal assessment - oral communication | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 15 min prep + 10 min exam | 25% |

strengths: Topic and research question are communicated clearly and consistently, with section headings that reinforce the investigationâs focus.
The research question is explicitly stated on the title page and reiterated in the body, anchoring the analysis.
The overall structure and sequencing of thematic sections demonstrate a coherent plan that aligns with the stated focus.
weaknesses: The methodology is only implied through structure and choice of close-reading; there is no explicit account of source-selection criteria or research procedures.
Occasional digressions into broader authorial intention slightly dilute the central focus on violence and identity.
Repetitive explanatory asides (e.g. reader reactions) detract from methodological sharpness.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The investigation exhibits a clearly articulated purpose, a well-focused research question, and a coherent plan reflected in its tripartite structure. However, the lack of an explicit methodological statement or discussion of how and why secondary sources were selected prevents it from reaching the very top band. On balance, the work meets the descriptors for a score of 5 in Focus and Method.

strengths: Literary and psychological terminology (e.g. imagery, foreshadowing, hyperbole, fight-or-flight) is used accurately to support the analysis.
Primary quotations are well chosen and integrated to illustrate the theme of violence shaping identity.
Secondary sources (Grossman, Nietzsche) are applied appropriately to enrich the reading and demonstrate interdisciplinary engagement.
weaknesses: The range of secondary literature is narrow, with little engagement with literary-critical scholarship or cultural/historical context.
Some arguments rely on general assertions without deeper theoretical grounding (e.g. claims about symbolism or realism).
Informal language slips (âstuff,â âharshnessâ) occasionally undermine academic tone.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay demonstrates clear and coherent knowledge of the primary text, appropriate use of terminology, and relevant application of secondary material. It lacks breadth of scholarly engagement and deeper contextualization, placing it firmly in the middle of the 3â4 band for Knowledge and Understanding.

strengths: Analytical sections consistently link textual evidence to thematic claims, showing depth of interpretation (e.g. resilience vs. vulnerability analyses).
The argument is structured around a clear three-part evaluation, and each section returns to the research question.
Insightful connections between narrative pacing, imagery, and psychological theory demonstrate strong critical engagement.
weaknesses: Engagement with alternative interpretations or counterarguments is limited, reducing critical breadth.
Some evaluative comments reiterate earlier analysis rather than advancing new insights.
Source evaluation and discussion of limitations (e.g. of secondary materials) are minimal.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essay exhibits good to very good critical thinking: analysis is generally coherent and well supported, and the discussion advances a focused argument. While it lacks the consistent excellence and multiplicity of perspectives of the topmost band, its sustained relevance to the research question and effective support of conclusions warrant a strong mid-to-upper-band score of 6 in Critical Thinking.

strengths: The reportâs organisation (title page, table of contents, clear subheadings, introduction, conclusion, bibliography) is complete and aids navigation.
Consistent formatting (font, heading hierarchy) and inclusion of required academic elements demonstrate attention to presentation standards.
Paragraphing and signposting are generally effective, supporting the flow of the argument.
weaknesses: Inconsistent citation markers (superscripts without linked footnotes/endnotes) suggest minor layout oversights.
Some paragraphs are overly dense and could be subdivided for clarity and readability.
A few abrupt paragraph endings hint at incomplete transitions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The essayâs overall structure is clearly appropriate and complete, satisfying the highest band descriptors for Presentation. Minor formatting lapses and occasional text-density issues prevent a perfectly polished submission, but the work nonetheless merits the top score of 4 in this criterion.

strandSelections.reasoning:
**Explicit statement**: The research questionâ"How does R.F. Kuang use the theme of violence to shape Rin's identity in The Poppy War?"âis explicitly presented on the title page and repeated in the body, demonstrating clarity.

**Focus and linkage**: Each analytic section returns to the RQ, consistently relating textual evidence to the notion of violence shaping identity. The sub-headings map directly onto facets of the question (resilience, vulnerability, moral evolution), evidencing purposeful focus.

**Minor limitations**: At times the argument stretches into broader commentary on readersâ reactions or general moral philosophy, slightly diffusing the tightness of focus. Nonetheless, the RQ remains **clearly stated, focused and effectively connected** to the ensuing discussion, warranting a 5 rather than the absolute top of the band.

**Quality of analysis**: The student moves beyond description by dissecting how specific quotations illustrate facets of resilience, vulnerability, and moral decay. Links between textual evidence and psychological theory (e.g., Grossmanâs conditioning) add depth.

**Coherence and insight**: Reasoning is generally logical, and paragraph structure supports a developing argument. The essay recognises irony and hypocrisy in Rinâs choices, demonstrating some critical nuance. A few assertions are repetitive or lack counter-argument, and transitions between sections can be smoother, preventing the analysis from attaining the "excellent" standard.

Overall, the work shows **good depth with minor inconsistencies**, justifying a mid-band score of 5 within the 4-6 range.

**Relevance and integration**: Primary quotations are well selected and unpacked, demonstrating clear links to the argument about violence and identity. Secondary materials (Grossman on psychology, Nietzsche on morality) are referenced to extend analysis, showing an effort to situate the reading within broader discourse.

**Depth and balance**: While the chosen sources support key points, the range is limited (only three secondary works, two of which are general rather than literary-critical). Counter-readings or scholarly debate on Kuangâs text are absent, so engagement remains **mostly** rather than consistently relevant and appropriate.

Overall, application exceeds the basic level but does not display the breadth or sustained critical synthesis characteristic of the top band, justifying a **score of 4 in the 3-4 band**.

**Formal requirements met**: The student provides title, subject identification, and an accurate word count within 4 000 words. Headings, sub-headings, and consistent font usage demonstrate attention to layout. The bibliography follows a recognisable citation style.

**Minor lapses**: Page numbers are referenced in the contents but are not visible in the supplied extract; in places superscript citation markers (e.g., \<sup>1\>) lack corresponding reference notes, hinting at formatting or referencing inconsistencies.

Overall, mostâbut not allâlayout conventions appear correctly applied, aligning with the **1-2 band descriptor of some minor issues**, yet the majority of criteria are met; therefore a score of 3 (lower end of 3-4 band) is awarded.

**Demonstrated understanding**: The student recounts key plot events accurately and analyses their psychological and thematic implications, indicating a solid grasp of Kuangâs narrative, character arcs, and major motifs.

**Coherence and insight**: Interpretation of Rinâs psychological evolution, use of literary devices (imagery, foreshadowing, hyperbole), and moral complexity is generally cogent and coherent. However, some sections verge on extended paraphrase of plot, and historical or cultural contexts of Chinese history and mythology receive minimal treatment.

Consequently, the essay reflects **clear but not consistently nuanced knowledge**, aligning with the upper level of the 3-4 band (score 4).

**Logical organisation**: The essay follows a traditional academic structure (introduction, themed analytical sections, conclusion). A detailed table of contents and clear sub-headings aid navigation; the sequence of ideas generally supports the unfolding argument.

**Completeness and coherence**: Required elements (title page, contents, introduction, conclusion, bibliography) are present. Paragraphing and signposting are effective, though some sections are text-heavy and occasionally repetitive, suggesting opportunities for tighter editing.

Given that structure is **clearly appropriate and complete**, with only minor stylistic issues, the strand merits the top of the 3-4 bandâscore 4.

**Breadth and relevance**: The essay gathers sufficient primary evidence and supplements it with a handful of secondary sources that illuminate psychological and ethical dimensions of violence. These materials are clearly relevant to the RQ.

**Limitations**: The scope of research is modest for an EE; there is little engagement with existing literary scholarship on Kuang or with broader discussions in post-colonial or trauma studies that would have strengthened credibility. Source evaluation (origin, purpose, limitations) is minimal.

Thus, the research is **adequate and mostly relevant**, matching the descriptors of the 4-6 range but nearer its lower end, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

**Argument development**: The essay advances a clear, three-part discussion that returns to the RQ and culminates in a reasoned, if not fully balanced, conclusion. The student evaluates Rinâs moral choices, invoking Nietzsche and psychological research to question her justifications.

**Critical engagement**: Some reflective questioning (e.g., "How could she compare the lives lost?") shows awareness of complexity. Nevertheless, engagement with alternate readings or counter-evidence is limited, and the conclusion tends to affirm rather than interrogate earlier claims.

Given the coherent, largely effective discussion with occasional lapses in critical balance, the work fits the **upper half of the 4-6 band**, earning a 5.

**Evidence of planning and source selection**: The student outlines a tripartite structure in the introduction and applies sustained close-reading of the primary text. Three secondary sources (Grossman, Nietzsche, a psychology article) are chosen for thematic resonance with violence and trauma, showing **some informed selection**.

**Completeness**: The bibliography is formatted and the essay stays within word limit. However, there is little explicit explanation of research methods, criteria for source inclusion, or acknowledgment of alternative critical perspectives; the investigation relies predominantly on plot summary and quoted passages from the novel.

Given the presence of a workable but not fully articulated methodology, the work fits the **upper end of the 3-4 band**, meriting a 4.

**Use of literary terminology**: Terms such as "imagery," "foreshadowing," "hyperbole," "tone," and "narration" are employed accurately, and the student occasionally references psychological concepts like "fight or flight."

**Consistency and precision**: While generally correct, usage sometimes remains surface-level; for instance, statements about narrative technique or symbolism are asserted without deeper theoretical grounding. Some minor language slips and informal phrasings (âstuff,â âharshnessâ) detract from overall precision.

On balance, terminology is **mostly accurate and appropriate**, supporting analysis without marked sophistication, meriting a 4 in the 3-4 band.

annotations.comment: Title clearly stated at the start, fulfilling the formatting requirement for the investigation.
Research question is stated clearly and linked to the title, providing strong focus to the investigation.
Word count is within the 4000-word limit and clearly provided, meeting IB formatting requirements.
Table of contents is complete and well-structured, aiding navigation through the essay.
Page numbers are mentioned in the contents but actual pages lack visible numbering, hindering reader navigation.
Discussion of vulnerabilities lacks historical or cultural context of Chinese mythology and war, which would deepen subject knowledge.
Evaluation of Rinâs fear and helplessness is insightful but could be balanced with moments of agency to demonstrate analytical cohesion.
This sentence trails off, suggesting an incomplete thought; revise to ensure clarity and completeness.
A single extended paragraph contains multiple ideas; consider subdividing for clearer thematic progression.
Justification of the topicâs contemporary relevance effectively situates the research in a broader context.
Introduction lacks an explicit description of methodological approach or criteria for source selection; adding this would strengthen methodological transparency.
The outline specifies thematic sections but omits discussion of research methods and source evaluation, which would enhance methodological completeness.
The analysis correctly employs literary terminology (e.g., imagery, contrast) to discuss how violence functions as a continuous theme.
The section could be strengthened by addressing possible counter-interpretations of Rinâs resilience rather than presenting a single reading.
Insightful linkage of Rinâs self-inflicted burns to her survival instincts demonstrates strong analysis depth.
The shift from Rinâs hesitation to her active violence is well-analyzed through vivid imagery and narrative pacing.
The essayâs structure is clearly outlined, detailing the three analytical sections and guiding the reader through the argument.
Claiming violence is âblack and whiteâ lacks scholarly support; consider citing a literary theory or critical source to substantiate this assertion.
Reiterating the research question as a section header reinforces focus and reminds the reader of the essayâs purpose.
The introduction relies heavily on plot summary, limiting analytical depth; consider focusing more on critical framing of the theme and less on narrative retelling.
Primary text evidence is well-selected and integrated to illustrate Rinâs resilience in the face of violence.
This section would benefit from explicitly linking the analysis back to the research question to maintain clear focus.
Foreshadowing analysis skillfully connects early hesitation to Rinâs eventual atrocity, showcasing analytical insight.
Paragraph ends abruptly, indicating an incomplete thought or missing transition; revise for coherence.
Quotation of Nietzsche enriches philosophical depth and effectively underpins the moral complexity of Rinâs transformation.
The Nietzsche citation cites Dolson et al. (1908) without clarification of the edition or translator, compromising bibliographic accuracy.
Missing page number at the bottom of this page disrupts consistency and professional presentation.
The self-harm versus tool interpretation would benefit from supporting evidence from trauma studies or psychological research beyond the primary text.
Introducing âfight or flightâ without definition or citation limits clarity; include a reference to a psychology source to define this concept.
Integration of Grossmanâs theory provides effective interdisciplinary support for Rinâs conditioning to violence.
The Grossman citation is missing page numbers and conforms inconsistently to a citation style; ensure complete and uniform referencing.
Paragraphs here are lengthy and dense; breaking them into shorter units would improve readability and signposting.
The vivid portrayal of the Khurdalain massacre is well-interpreted, effectively illustrating Rinâs psychological transformation.
Evaluation in this section reiterates earlier analysis rather than offering new insight; consider synthesising points to avoid repetition.
E-book citation lacks publisher location and proper formatting of series title; verify against style guidelines.
The secondary reference to Potkanska via Muller (2016) should be traced to original research to verify accuracy and strengthen research quality.
Analysis of relational violence between Rin and Altan demonstrates good depth and links character dynamics to thematic vulnerabilities.
The quote from Potkanska lacks full source details in the bibliography, which undermines source reliability; verify and provide complete reference.
Bibliography entries demonstrate inconsistent formatting styles (e.g., italics and punctuation); adopt one citation style consistently.
Conclusion introduces the idea of ârealismâ in fantasy without prior development; ensure all thematic claims are supported in body paragraphs.
Web source entry for Psychology Today misses access date and page title; include full details to meet IB requirements.
